verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Cantinho da Baia After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.

conversationalSummary.text: Cantinho da Baia at After work (16:30 start) looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the lack of push could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 171 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 75/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay; gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk.
best.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is too weak and wrap-starved in the sheltered bay
gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 125 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 76/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks
gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy
